title: Listening - Part 2
description: For each question, choose the correct answer.
instructions: You will hear six short extracts. For questions 8–13, choose the answer (A, B or C) which fits best according to what you hear.

extracts.context: You will hear a boy telling a friend about plans for his birthday.
extracts.audio_script: Girl: Are you having a big party for your birthday? || Boy: Not really. I wanted to invite everyone from football and my cousins too, but the climbing centre only allows ten children in one group. || Girl: That's a shame. Are some people refusing to come? || Boy: No, everyone seems keen. [8]I'm just disappointed because I have to choose, and I'd like more friends to be there[/8]. It won't be boring, though. The place looks brilliant.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: Are you having a big party for your birthday?

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: Not really. I wanted to invite everyone from football and my cousins too, but the climbing centre only allows ten children in one group.

dialogue.speaker: girl
dialogue.text: That's a shame. Are some people refusing to come?

dialogue.speaker: boy
dialogue.text: No, everyone seems keen. I'm just disappointed because I have to choose, and I'd like more friends to be there. It won't be boring, though. The place looks brilliant.

questions.question: How does he feel about the plans he's made?

options.A: annoyed that some friends don't want to come
options.B: disappointed that he can't invite more friends
options.C: worried that it might be boring for his friends

questions.explanation: He says he is disappointed because he has to choose and wants more friends to attend.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about a football match they went to.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Did you enjoy the match yesterday? || Woman: It was exciting, especially near the end. I thought the stadium looked half empty, though. || Man: Yes, there weren't as many people as usual, probably because of the rain. || Woman: The players did well in those conditions. || Man: True, but [9]some of the referee's decisions were really poor. Even our coach looked furious[/9]. || Woman: I noticed that too. At least our team still won. || Man: I can see why. || Woman: Yes, it wasn't what I expected, but I learnt something useful.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Did you enjoy the match yesterday?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It was exciting, especially near the end. I thought the stadium looked half empty, though.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Yes, there weren't as many people as usual, probably because of the rain.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: The players did well in those conditions.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: True, but some of the referee's decisions were really poor. Even our coach looked furious.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I noticed that too. At least our team still won.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: I can see why.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, it wasn't what I expected, but I learnt something useful.

questions.question: They both think that

options.B: the crowd was larger than usual.
options.C: the referee made some bad decisions.

questions.explanation: Both speakers notice that the referee made poor decisions.

extracts.context: You will hear a man telling his friend about a skiing holiday.
extracts.audio_script: Woman: Your skiing photos looked fun. Did you enjoy the holiday? || Man: Parts of it, yes. I didn't get injured, luckily, although I nearly fell into a tree once. || Woman: You looked confident in the pictures. || Man: That's because my friends only posted the good ones. [10]Most of the time I was the slowest person in the group, and I felt really silly when everyone had to wait for me[/10]. || Woman: I'm sure they didn't mind.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Your skiing photos looked fun. Did you enjoy the holiday?

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Parts of it, yes. I didn't get injured, luckily, although I nearly fell into a tree once.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: You looked confident in the pictures.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That's because my friends only posted the good ones. Most of the time I was the slowest person in the group, and I felt really silly when everyone had to wait for me.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I'm sure they didn't mind.

questions.question: How did he feel during the holiday?

options.C: angry that his friends put photos online

questions.explanation: He says he was the slowest and felt really silly, so he was embarrassed about his ability.

extracts.context: You will hear two friends talking about cars.
extracts.audio_script: Man: You're still looking for a new car, aren't you? || Woman: Yes. I watched a programme last night, but it was mostly about racing cars, not ordinary ones. || Man: What about the adverts in magazines? || Woman: They're useful for prices, but they only say good things. [11]I prefer reading comments from drivers online because they explain what the cars are like after a few months[/11]. || Man: That sounds more honest. || Woman: Exactly. I'll use those before I decide.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: You're still looking for a new car, aren't you?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes. I watched a programme last night, but it was mostly about racing cars, not ordinary ones.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: What about the adverts in magazines?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: They're useful for prices, but they only say good things. I prefer reading comments from drivers online because they explain what the cars are like after a few months.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That sounds more honest.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Exactly. I'll use those before I decide.

questions.question: The woman thinks the best way to get information about new cars is from
questions.explanation: She says she prefers reading comments from drivers online.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman telling a friend about a singing competition.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Are you still helping with that singing competition? || Woman: Yes, and it's busier than I expected. I'm not choosing the winner, thankfully; that would be too difficult. || Man: Would you like to sing in it yourself? || Woman: Not at all. I enjoy being behind the scenes. [12]There are forms to check, singers to contact and rooms to book, so I'm spending nearly every evening organising it[/12]. || Man: You must be tired. || Woman: I am, but it'll be worth it.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Are you still helping with that singing competition?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Yes, and it's busier than I expected. I'm not choosing the winner, thankfully; that would be too difficult.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Would you like to sing in it yourself?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Not at all. I enjoy being behind the scenes. There are forms to check, singers to contact and rooms to book, so I'm spending nearly every evening organising it.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: You must be tired.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: I am, but it'll be worth it.

questions.question: What does the woman say about the competition?

options.A: It is taking a long time to organise.
options.B: Judging it is the easiest part.
options.C: She would love to perform in it.

questions.explanation: She says she is spending nearly every evening organising the competition.

extracts.context: You will hear a woman talking to a friend about her recent move to a city.
extracts.audio_script: Man: Are you getting used to city life yet? || Woman: Mostly. The street noise still wakes me sometimes, and I don't know many people in the building. || Man: Do you miss the countryside? || Woman: A little, but there are good things too. [13]I can walk to the shops, the library and the park in a few minutes, so this neighbourhood really works well for me[/13]. || Man: That sounds convenient. || Woman: It is. I'm glad I chose this area.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Are you getting used to city life yet?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: Mostly. The street noise still wakes me sometimes, and I don't know many people in the building.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: Do you miss the countryside?

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: A little, but there are good things too. I can walk to the shops, the library and the park in a few minutes, so this neighbourhood really works well for me.

dialogue.speaker: man
dialogue.text: That sounds convenient.

dialogue.speaker: woman
dialogue.text: It is. I'm glad I chose this area.

questions.question: How does the woman feel about her move?

options.A: pleased about a surprising health benefit
options.C: glad that she has met friendly people

questions.explanation: She says the neighbourhood works well for her and she is glad she chose the area.
